codeReview.summary: The follow-up fix at current HEAD closes the two round-3 gaps: `checkSimpleCommand()` now walks `cmd.assignments`, and `walkWordParts()` now descends through nested `ParameterExpansion`/`BraceExpansion` words with matching regression coverage. But the wrapper still leaves one executable substring-arithmetic shape unchecked: `walkArithExpr()` only rejects `ArithCommandSubst` and treats `ArithBracedExpansion` as safe, so `${FOO:0:${BAR:-$(whoami)}}` can still smuggle command substitution under an allow-listed head.

issues.description: `walkArithExpr()` treats `ArithBracedExpansion` as a safe leaf. just-bash parses `ls ${FOO:0:${BAR:-$(whoami)}}` as a `Substring` whose `length.expression.type === 'ArithBracedExpansion'` with raw content `BAR:-$(whoami)`. Because `checkSimpleCommand()` delegates substring offset/length validation only to this arithmetic walker, the wrapper accepts an allow-listed `ls` invocation even though `${BAR:-$(whoami)}` executes `$(whoami)` before `ls` runs. This reopens a real allow-list bypass the pre-AST scanner would have rejected on raw `$(`, and the current regression suite only covers the direct `ArithCommandSubst` form (`${FOO:0:$(...)}`), not this braced-expansion arithmetic variant.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture note now overstates substring-arithmetic coverage. It says the current walker covers `Substring.offset` / `.length` by rejecting `ArithCommandSubst`, but just-bash also represents `${...}` inside arithmetic as `ArithBracedExpansion`, which can carry raw `$(...)` text and is currently uninspected.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/trieloff/Developer/slicc/.yolo/droid-2/.factory/library/architecture.md:66-75 says the current allow-list walker covers all security-sensitive substring arithmetic nodes via `ArithCommandSubst`; /Users/trieloff/Developer/slicc/.yolo/droid-2/packages/webapp/src/tools/bash-tool-allowlist.ts:514-574 skips `ArithBracedExpansion` in `walkArithExpr()`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared guidance still conflicts on whether pure utility-wrapper features must perform live-browser validation. Both the original rewrite and the follow-up fix skipped CDP checks in their handoffs following the webapp-worker skill, while the shared user-testing doc still says workers and validators MUST run the live-browser surface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/trieloff/Developer/slicc/.yolo/droid-2/.factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md:94-98 makes CDP verification optional for pure utility wrappers; /Users/trieloff/Developer/slicc/.yolo/droid-2/.factory/library/user-testing.md:19-25 says workers and validators MUST do live-browser validation; /Users/trieloff/.factory/missions/094c0314-6896-4f76-bf58-89b7dfee66f4/handoffs/2026-04-16T23-44-40-237Z__rewrite-bash-allowlist-on-just-bash-ast__1213770f-2849-436f-b0d7-d22539e6acd9.json:12 and :61 plus /Users/trieloff/.factory/missions/094c0314-6896-4f76-bf58-89b7dfee66f4/handoffs/2026-04-17T00-31-21-332Z__fix-allowlist-assignment-and-nested-word-bypasses__ba640e88-a11c-489b-a536-93c06ed54a1a.json:56 both show empty `interactiveChecks`.

summary: Fail. Current HEAD resolves the round-3 blockers around `cmd.assignments` and nested parameter/brace word traversal, but the allow-list walker still trusts `ArithBracedExpansion` inside substring arithmetic. That leaves a surviving bypass such as `ls ${FOO:0:${BAR:-$(whoami)}}`, so the original feature cannot yet be considered passing in combination with the follow-up fix.
